title: Secretary's Day

jokes.text: Have you seen them? Wait, I'm sitting on them.
jokes.setup: Character asks Oscar if he's seen her Girl Scout cookies
jokes.punchline: Realizes she's been sitting on them the whole time
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup with efficient delivery, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: What's the difference between a chimichanga, a chalupa and a tostada? Call me back ASAP. It's urgent.
jokes.setup: Character treats Mexican food terminology as an urgent matter
jokes.punchline: The contrast between 'ASAP' urgency and completely trivial question
jokes.explanation: Great economy and absurdist premise - treating mundane as urgent is classic Office humor

jokes.text: Wait, this isn't funny. I don't talk like that.
jokes.setup: Kevin is being mocked with Cookie Monster impression
jokes.punchline: Kevin's defensive reaction reveals his self-awareness about the comparison
jokes.explanation: Strong character integration - Kevin's wounded dignity is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Say, 'Me eat cookie.' No. I won't say it.
jokes.setup: Someone pressures Kevin to do Cookie Monster voice
jokes.punchline: Kevin's stubborn refusal maintains his dignity
jokes.explanation: Kevin's wounded pride creates good cringe comedy, economically delivered

jokes.text: Break it up, you clique.
jokes.setup: People gathered around accounting watching Kevin video
jokes.punchline: 'Clique' instead of 'click' - both work contextually
jokes.explanation: Subtle wordplay that works on multiple levels but might be missed

jokes.text: Is that the program where all those puppets live in the barrio?
jokes.setup: Someone explains Kevin looks like Cookie Monster from Sesame Street
jokes.punchline: Describing Sesame Street as 'puppets in the barrio' is both accurate and hilariously clinical
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reframing of beloved children's show through corporate lens - very Office-style humor

jokes.text: It's Secretary's Day. And it is Erin's and my three-week anniversary, so perfect storm for a romantic gesture.
jokes.setup: Andy explains why today is special
jokes.punchline: Calling a three-week anniversary a 'perfect storm' for romance is absurdly overwrought
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - his tendency to oversell minor romantic milestones

jokes.text: I sent an email blast, a text blast, a good old-fashioned talk blast.
jokes.setup: Andy lists ways he reminded people about Secretary's Day
jokes.punchline: 'Talk blast' is not a real thing but fits the pattern
jokes.explanation: Good escalation pattern with made-up term that sounds plausible

jokes.text: I sent a snail-mail blast a week ago, and a stern reminder via fax at 7:00 this morning, so people better step up and appreciate the crap out of Erin.
jokes.setup: Continuation of Andy's communication methods
jokes.punchline: 'Appreciate the crap out of Erin' is aggressively wholesome
jokes.explanation: Andy's over-the-top enthusiasm expressed through crude language creates good character comedy

jokes.text: If it wasn't for secretaries, I wouldn't have a stepmom.
jokes.setup: Character explaining importance of secretaries
jokes.punchline: Reveals personal family drama through workplace appreciation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - reveals character backstory through unexpected angle

jokes.text: What was maternity leave like? Oh. How do I explain it? It rocked. It rocked my ass off.
jokes.setup: Pam pauses thoughtfully about describing maternity leave
jokes.punchline: Uses unexpectedly crude enthusiasm to describe motherhood
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectation - Pam's uncharacteristic crude enthusiasm

jokes.text: You couldn't have watered it? I literally did not know that existed until this moment. Well, I knew it existed, and I chose to let it die.
jokes.setup: Jim questions Pam about dead plant
jokes.punchline: Pam's admission of willful plant murder is unexpectedly dark
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Pam's new motherhood priorities in darkly comic way

jokes.text: She's kind of a rube. That's my girlfriend you're talking about.
jokes.setup: Dwight casually insults Erin to Andy
jokes.punchline: Andy's delayed defensive reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with good character work from both parties

jokes.text: I got a picture of you asking me to lunch.
jokes.setup: Dwight asks Erin to lunch
jokes.punchline: Erin's non-sequitur response about taking a picture
jokes.explanation: Shows Erin's childlike wonder and strange priorities perfectly

jokes.text: Well, happy boss's day. There wouldn't be a secretary if there was no boss.
jokes.setup: Dwight wishes Erin happy Secretary's Day
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent but logically flawed response
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's naive but earnest logic

jokes.text: Yeah, ha, ha, tray of cookies. I'm not falling for that. I brought those in. It's my birthday.
jokes.setup: Kevin assumes cookies are another Cookie Monster joke
jokes.punchline: They're actually Darryl's birthday cookies, making Kevin look paranoid
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Kevin's paranoia while creating awkward moment

jokes.text: That's some stone-cold narcissism right there.
jokes.setup: Kevin accuses Darryl of participating in Cookie Monster mockery
jokes.punchline: Darryl's dry assessment of Kevin's self-centeredness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Darryl line - clinical observation delivered with characteristic deadpan

jokes.text: They're making fun of Cookie Monster. I get that. But in a strange way, it feels like they're making fun of me.
jokes.setup: Kevin reflects on the Cookie Monster parody
jokes.punchline: His complete lack of self-awareness about why the comparison exists
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kevin moment - his wounded confusion is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oscar, did you eat some of my M&M's? The level...
jokes.setup: Kevin obsessively monitoring his candy levels
jokes.punchline: The implication he measures M&M levels like a scientist
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Kevin's food obsession through scientific approach

jokes.text: This is violent and offensive.
jokes.setup: Kevin describing the Cookie Monster video
jokes.punchline: Calling a harmless parody 'violent and offensive' is absurd overreaction
jokes.explanation: Kevin's dramatic overreaction perfectly captures his wounded pride

jokes.text: Either they don't respect me, or they respect me too much. And some of them still think that I'm the IT guy.
jokes.setup: Gabe analyzing his workplace relationships
jokes.punchline: Both his contradictory analysis and the IT guy confusion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe moment - his analytical overthinking and general irrelevance

jokes.text: And that's straight from corporate, so... Not to be scary, but, yeah, I would listen to me.
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to assert authority over Cookie Monster video
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at being threatening while immediately undermining himself
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Gabe's complete lack of natural authority

jokes.text: This is a novelization of the movie Precious, Based on the Book Push by Sapphire.
jokes.setup: Erin wants to listen to audiobook in car
jokes.punchline: The absurd recursive nature of movie-to-book-to-audiobook adaptation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment that perfectly captures Erin's naivety about media consumption

jokes.text: I would have chosen the 1490s. 'Cause America was discovered.
jokes.setup: Dwight asks what decade she'd want to be a teenager in
jokes.punchline: Erin's historically naive response about 'discovery' of America
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Erin's simplistic view of history

jokes.text: And then my last job was at a Taco Bell Express, but then it became a full Taco Bell and, I don't know, I couldn't keep up.
jokes.setup: Erin describing her work history
jokes.punchline: Implies the transition from Express to full Taco Bell was too demanding
jokes.explanation: Excellent Erin moment - the absurd implication that a full menu was overwhelming

jokes.text: At my foster home I never had a desk, so it's like... I don't mean that I didn't like my foster home. I did like it. I just didn't have a desk there.
jokes.setup: Erin explaining why having a desk is special
jokes.punchline: Her defensive backtracking reveals insecurity about her background
jokes.explanation: Poignant character moment that creates both humor and sympathy

jokes.text: Yeah, like a favorite month. Like, I liked April when I was seven.
jokes.setup: Dwight asks if she had a favorite age or month
jokes.punchline: Erin's oddly specific and childlike response
jokes.explanation: Shows Erin's childlike way of thinking about time and preferences

jokes.text: Erin is just weird. How many pillows do you sleep on at night?
jokes.setup: Dwight's talking head assessment
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by his own weird question to Erin
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic juxtaposition - Dwight calling Erin weird while being equally weird

jokes.text: Other than the fact that he dated Angela, I don't think he is a snappy dresser.
jokes.setup: Dwight describing Andy's good qualities
jokes.punchline: Casually drops major relationship bombshell as throwaway detail
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - major plot point delivered as casual aside

jokes.text: What? Sorry. About Angela. Did you say he dated Angela?
jokes.setup: Erin processes what Dwight just revealed
jokes.punchline: Her delayed, escalating realization of the bombshell
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Erin's processing of shocking information

jokes.text: Yeah, I asked for pickles on my burger, and there are only, like, five or six. Can I get some more pickles?
jokes.setup: Erin is upset about Andy's secret but addresses waiter
jokes.punchline: She displaces her emotional distress onto pickle quantity
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing how people redirect emotional pain

jokes.text: In the foster home, my hair was my room.
jokes.setup: Erin having breakdown and pulling her hair over her face
jokes.punchline: Heartbreaking explanation that's both funny and sad
jokes.explanation: Exceptional line - heartbreaking backstory delivered with perfect economy and genuine pathos

jokes.text: I'll have what she's having.
jokes.setup: Erin having public breakdown in restaurant
jokes.punchline: Classic When Harry Met Sally reference in completely wrong context
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that works because the context is completely inappropriate

jokes.text: Her milk is coming in. She's getting uncomfortable.
jokes.setup: Pam appears uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Dwight's clinical diagnosis of postpartum lactation
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight moment - inappropriate but accurate biological observation

jokes.text: Same thing happens with my cows if I don't tend them frequently enough. You gotta milk them, or else they'll moo like crazy.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining Pam's discomfort
jokes.punchline: Comparing Pam to his farm animals in clinical detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight escalation - making human-animal comparison with complete sincerity

jokes.text: Really? Fine. Let your breasts explode. Three squeezes and I would drain you.
jokes.setup: Pam refuses Dwight's help with breast pump situation
jokes.punchline: Dwight's inappropriately confident offer of lactation assistance
jokes.explanation: Peak cringe Dwight moment - inappropriate confidence in his farm-based expertise

jokes.text: I just like the way it feels. Just relax, okay? This is like the Cadillac of pumps.
jokes.setup: Meredith caught using Pam's breast pump
jokes.punchline: Her casual enjoyment and product review of stolen breast pump
jokes.explanation: Perfect Meredith moment - completely inappropriate behavior treated casually

jokes.text: We're both girls. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Pam objects to sharing breast pump
jokes.punchline: Meredith's casual dismissal of obvious hygiene concerns
jokes.explanation: Good Meredith logic - inappropriate boundary-crossing with flawed reasoning

jokes.text: God, Oscar, will you keep your pants on? It's easy.
jokes.setup: Oscar asks for fax cover sheets
jokes.punchline: Kevin's completely disproportionate annoyance at simple request
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment showing his irritability when interrupted from Cookie Monster focus

jokes.text: 'Michael, can I have an advance on my paycheck, because a Mrs. Fields Cookies just opened up at the mall!'
jokes.setup: Jim doing Kevin Cookie Monster impression
jokes.punchline: Perfect Kevin dialogue combining his food obsession with financial irresponsibility
jokes.explanation: Exceptional impression that perfectly captures both Kevin's voice and priorities

jokes.text: 'Sorry, Gabe, but that show hasn't been on in many cookies.'
jokes.setup: Jim continuing Kevin impression after Gabe mentions MADtv
jokes.punchline: Replacing time measurement with cookies in Kevin-speak
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that maintains Cookie Monster voice while making grammatical sense

jokes.text: that 115-pound moonbeam over there named Erin Hannon
jokes.setup: Andy introducing Erin at Secretary's Day party
jokes.punchline: Overly romantic description that includes her exact weight
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - romantic gesture undermined by inappropriate detail

jokes.text: I know about Angela. I know that you were engaged to her and that you were sleeping with her. Michael told me.
jokes.setup: Andy about to perform romantic song
jokes.punchline: Erin's public confrontation ruins the moment completely
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe timing - romantic gesture destroyed by relationship revelation

jokes.text: Did you sleep with Phyllis or Kelly or Pam? Maybe all together?
jokes.setup: Erin confronting Andy about Angela
jokes.punchline: Her innocent mind going to absurd group scenario
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Erin's naive understanding of workplace relationships

jokes.text: I should probably get involved in this, but I think my energy is better spent on the Cookie Monster issue.
jokes.setup: Andy and Erin having public relationship crisis
jokes.punchline: Gabe prioritizes stupid internet video over human drama
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe moment - missing the point of real human needs for bureaucratic concerns

jokes.text: 'Hey, Erin, you look delicious! I mean, beautiful.'
jokes.setup: Someone doing Cookie Monster impression
jokes.punchline: Mixing romantic compliment with food-focused slip
jokes.explanation: Decent callback that combines ongoing joke with romantic context

jokes.text: I'm going to have to suspend you without pay for two days. You can't just suspend someone from work. Yeah, you're right. You know what? You're suspended too, Jim.
jokes.setup: Gabe trying to assert authority over Cookie Monster joke
jokes.punchline: His authority crumbles immediately, then he doubles down absurdly
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Gabe's complete lack of actual authority

jokes.text: Wait, what? I was just slow-clapping your no-nonsense decision-making.
jokes.setup: Gabe suspends Dwight too
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confusion at being punished for supporting Gabe
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight moment - his literal-minded confusion at illogical punishment

jokes.text: 'C' is for suspension.
jokes.setup: Gabe suspending everyone over Cookie Monster jokes
jokes.punchline: Cookie Monster voice making light of the suspension
jokes.explanation: Clever callback using Cookie Monster's 'C is for Cookie' format

jokes.text: That's like... That's... That's like Mr. & Mrs. Smith crap.
jokes.setup: Dwight criticizing Andy for keeping secrets from Erin
jokes.punchline: Comparing workplace romance secret to spy movie
jokes.explanation: Decent Dwight pop culture reference but not particularly clever or original

jokes.text: Everyone here knows that I can't and won't keep a secret.
jokes.setup: Dwight defending himself for revealing Andy's secret
jokes.punchline: His proud admission of being completely untrustworthy
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware Dwight moment - turning character flaw into badge of honor

jokes.text: It's the kind of thing you wish you could have annulled.
jokes.setup: Angela talking about relationship with Andy
jokes.punchline: Her disgust so complete she wants to legally undo it
jokes.explanation: Good Angela moment - her characteristic cruelty expressed through legal terminology

jokes.text: Sometimes the heart doesn't know what it wants until it finds what it wants.
jokes.setup: Pam giving Erin relationship advice
jokes.punchline: Accidentally circular and meaningless wisdom delivered with conviction
jokes.explanation: Good moment showing how people offer empty platitudes when trying to help

jokes.text: And I gotta take this bad attitude and I gotta go home and I got to adjust it.
jokes.setup: Jim sarcastically accepting his suspension
jokes.punchline: Mock-serious commitment to attitude adjustment
jokes.explanation: Decent Jim sarcasm but not particularly memorable or clever

jokes.text: I kneel before you. Humbly taking your hand upon which I kiss to show my contrition.
jokes.setup: Dwight demonstrating how to properly apologize to Gabe
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top medieval submission display
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight over-commitment to authority that goes way too far

jokes.text: I mean, yeah, my chest is not naturally hairless, and my parents pay my credit card bill.
jokes.setup: Andy insisting he has no more secrets
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals two more embarrassing secrets
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment showing his complete lack of filter when nervous

jokes.text: Like that girl Precious in Precious, Based on the Novel Push by Sapphire.
jokes.setup: Erin saying she needs to be alone
jokes.punchline: References her earlier audiobook with full pretentious title
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Erin's tendency to over-cite media references

jokes.text: Or cookies!
jokes.setup: Gabe offering to buy everyone coffee after suspension disaster
jokes.punchline: His attempt to join in Cookie Monster joke falls completely flat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe moment - his awkward attempt at humor after completely mishandling situation

jokes.text: 'Guys, I'm now going to apologize for the mess that we're in, because corporate told me to. I just want you to know, I laugh like a crazy person.'
jokes.setup: Someone doing Gabe impression
jokes.punchline: Perfect mimicry of his awkward corporate-speak and weird laugh
jokes.explanation: Good impression that captures Gabe's corporate awkwardness and self-awareness

jokes.text: 'I have to go back to the zoo to the stick insect exhibit.'
jokes.setup: Continuing Gabe impression
jokes.punchline: Comparing Gabe's appearance to stick insects
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy reference to Gabe's extremely thin appearance

jokes.text: 'I say ciao 'cause I'm fancy from Tallahassee.'
jokes.setup: Continuing Gabe impression
jokes.punchline: Mocking his pretentious language for someone from Florida
jokes.explanation: Good observation about Gabe's pretentious vocabulary vs. unglamorous origins

jokes.text: She's old enough to be your mom, for one thing, and she's, like, three feet tall, and she wears pioneer women clothing, and I don't think she's ever pooped in her life.
jokes.setup: Michael reassuring Erin about Angela
jokes.punchline: List of Angela's flaws escalates to absurd biological claim
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that perfectly captures Angela's uptight personality through absurd biological observation

jokes.text: Andy, his butt looks big in those khakis. Oh, I like his butt. You said butt. You tricked me.
jokes.setup: Michael insulting Andy's appearance to make Erin feel better
jokes.punchline: Erin's confession reveals she still cares, then realizes she was tricked
jokes.explanation: Good emotional manipulation that reveals Erin's true feelings despite her anger

jokes.text: And the Count! Nailed him. Nailed him! Good work, Cookie Monster.
jokes.setup: Kevin sees Oscar's Count impression video
jokes.punchline: Kevin genuinely praising impression while still being called Cookie Monster
jokes.explanation: Decent callback showing Kevin's forgiving nature despite ongoing mockery

tmdb_overview: Andy celebrates Erin; Michael reveals Andy's relationship with Angela.
